heading: Sixto stage graph now maps into the CCH frame with no nontrivial exact QA/RT law landing on recovered geometry
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, sixto-ramos, cch-frame-map, rational-trig, geometry-test]
body: Built `build_sixto_frame_map_and_law_tests.py` and generated `sixto_cch_frame_map.json` plus `sixto_qa_rt_landing_tests.json`. The strict baseline is a center-similarity map from the recovered stage graph into the recovered `cch_archm1b` primitive frame using stage 1 -> left red primary circle and stage 2 -> central orange circle. That baseline gives similarity scale `0.933631`, rotation `24.666086°`, stage-1 radius residual `-12.650326`, stage-2 radius residual `121.476387`, and stage-3 / stage-4 nearest-primitive gaps `800.99939` and `1555.963481`. The anchor line is not the dominant red axis: angle gap `7.680926°`, spread `0.017864`. The secondary K-rule projection preserves the source `x 0.62` rule but still misses the orange radius by `133.92732`. Exact QA/RT landing tests are negative across the packet: `34/55`, `55/89`, and `89/144` are all close to but not exactly `0.62`, and the candidate primitive triangles fail exact quadrance-Pythagoras. Honest verdict: no nontrivial QA/RT law lands exactly on the recovered image geometry; the only exact equalities are the anchor coincidences imposed by the frame map itself.
